Neptune r5d Instance Type Family and Lookup Cache Feature

Neptune has a lookup-cache feature.
The lookup-cache feature can be used to improve the performance of queries which need to fetch and return large numbers of property values and literals.
The lookup-cache feature is used primarily by customers with queries that need to return many attributes.
The lookup cache boosts performance of queries by fetching attribute values locally rather than looking up each one over and over in Neptune indexed storage.
The lookup cache is implemented using a NVMe-attached EBS volume on an `r5d` instance type.
The lookup cache is enabled using a cluster's parameter group.
As data is fetched from Neptune indexed storage, property values and RDF literals are cached within the NVMe volume.
The `r5d` instance type is a Neptune instance type.
The `r5` instance type is a Neptune instance type.
If the lookup cache feature is not needed, a standard `r5` instance type should be used rather than an `r5d` instance type.
Using an `r5` instance type rather than an `r5d` instance type avoids the higher cost of the `r5d`.
The `r5d` family has instance types in the same sizes as the `r5` family.
The `r5d` family ranges in size from `r5d.large` to `r5d.24xlarge`.
The `r5` family ranges in size from `r5.large` to `r5.24xlarge`.
